- [Portrait of a Young Woman by Winold Reiss Framed Matte Canvas Portrait - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/portrait-of-a-young-woman-by-winold-reiss-framed-matte-canvas-portrait): Portrait of a Young Woman exemplifies Winold Reiss’s modern approach to portraiture and his commitment to representing individuality with dignity. Born in Germany and later establishing his career in the United States, Reiss combined European modernist training with a deep interest in cultural identity. This work reflects his sensitivity to form and psychology, using clean contours, restrained colour, and a simplified background to emphasise the sitter’s profile and introspective expression. The painting captures a quiet strength and timeless elegance, illustrating Reiss’s belief that portraiture should reveal character rather than social status. A matte canvas print with a slim black frame that holds a quiet, intimate portrait of a woman. The muted background and soft palette draw you to the subject’s thoughtful profile — the rhythm of her neckline, the textured stroke of her hair, the lace at her sleeve. This framed canvas brings gallery-quality detail to a living room wall, a hallway, or a focused nook where calm, reflection, and subtle style are welcome. It’s made with durable cotton-poly canvas and archival-grade latex inks so the tones stay true and the surface stays matte and glare-free. Arrives ready to hang with sawtooth hardware and a sustainably sourced pine frame for a refined, responsible finish.Product features- Greenguard Gold UL- inks for high-quality, non-toxic color- Cotton–polyester canvas composite with proprietary matte coating- Sustainably sourced pine frame, multiple sizes and frame colors available- Durable, non-hazardous latex inks producing vibrant, accurate tones- Includes sawtooth hanging hardware; horizontal, vertical, and square optionsCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Portrait of a Young Woman by Winold Reiss Portrait Canvas Print – Matte Stretched Wall Art - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/portrait-of-a-young-woman-by-winold-reiss-portrait-canvas-print-matte-stretched-wall-art): Winold Reiss, a German-born American artist known for his influential portrait work, created Portrait of a Young Woman as a study in balance, line, and human presence. Trained in European art traditions before emigrating to America, Reiss developed a distinctive style that merged realism with modernist clarity. In this painting, the controlled palette and refined draftsmanship draw attention to the sitter’s calm profile and composed posture. The absence of narrative detail allows the viewer to focus on personality and inner life, a hallmark of Reiss’s humanistic approach to portraiture. A matte stretched canvas that brings quiet, thoughtful portraiture into your home. The subtly textured surface and muted palette invite a slow, intimate gaze—like finding a paused moment in a sunlit studio. Leaning against a neutral wall, this canvas reads like a gentle conversation piece: calm, dignified, and quietly expressive. It suits spaces where stillness and reflection matter—bedrooms, reading nooks, or a pared-back living room. The slim 1.25" profile keeps the piece contemporary and gallery-ready, while the soft rubber dots and included back hanging make installation simple and stable.Product features- Matte, stretched cotton-poly canvas with a 1.25" gallery profile- Printed with UL- Greenguard Gold latex inks for high-quality, non-toxic color- Stretched over renewable radiata pine frame; soft rubber back dots prevent slipping- Available in multiple orientations and 60 size options; back-hanging hardware included- Small size tolerance due to handmade stretching process (+/- 1/8" / 3.2 mm)Care instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Portrait of an African American Woman by Winold Reiss Framed Matte Canvas Portrait - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/portrait-of-an-african-american-woman-by-winold-reiss-framed-matte-canvas-portrait): Portrait of an African American Woman reflects Winold Reiss’s progressive vision and deep commitment to portraying cultural identity with dignity. Born in Germany and trained in European modernist traditions, Reiss emigrated to the United States in the early twentieth century, where he became known for his respectful depictions of diverse communities. This painting combines strong graphic design with careful observation, using bold colour, patterned textiles, and a direct frontal pose to emphasise presence and individuality. Reiss’s approach challenged prevailing stereotypes of his time, presenting the sitter with confidence, humanity, and modern elegance. Hand-stretched matte canvas with a sleek black frame that lets bold portraiture breathe. The muted cream background and warm, earthy tones of the subject’s skin and outfit are rendered with vibrant, archival inks so every brushstroke, shadow and subtle hue holds its presence on the wall. Lightweight but sturdy, this framed canvas arrives ready to hang and brings a quietly powerful, intimate energy to living rooms, studies, and gallery walls.Hang it where conversations start — above a sofa, beside a bookshelf, or in an entry that greets guests with calm confidence. The refined matte surface reduces glare while preserving texture, making the portrait feel like an original piece rather than just a print. For collectors of modern figurative art and anyone who favors soulful, person-centered work, this framed canvas adds warmth and character without shouting for attention.Product features- Greenguard Gold  inks for high-quality, low-emission prints- Durable cotton-polyester canvas with proprietary matte coating- Sustainably sourced pinewood frame available in multiple colors- Latex inks: non-toxic, non-flammable, and vivid color reproduction- Comes ready to hang with sawtooth hardware; multiple sizes and orientations availableCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Portrait of an African American Woman by Winold Reiss Portrait Canvas Print — Matte Stretched - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/portrait-of-an-african-american-woman-by-winold-reiss-portrait-canvas-print-matte-stretched): Winold Reiss was a German-born American artist whose work reshaped modern portraiture in the United States. Portrait of an African American Woman exemplifies his belief that portraiture should honour identity rather than status. Influenced by European modernism and American realism, Reiss employed simplified forms, rich colour, and clear contours to create a powerful visual presence. The striking garment and composed expression draw the viewer’s focus to character and self-possession. Created during a period of growing cultural awareness, the painting stands as a testament to Reiss’s humanistic ideals and inclusive artistic vision. This stretched matte canvas brings a quiet, confident portrait into your space with tactile warmth and museum-ready presence. Printed with UL- Greenguard Gold latex inks, the image keeps rich, true colors without chemical fumes. The 1.25" pine profile gives the piece subtle depth on the wall, while the soft rubber dots on the back protect surfaces and steady the frame. It hangs ready — vertical, horizontal, or square — and arrives crafted from sustainable radiata pine and a sturdy cotton-poly canvas that feels both durable and refined. The result is a soulful artwork that sits comfortably in living rooms, studios, or curated gallery walls, adding a personal, contemplative focal point that ages gracefully with your space.Product features- High-quality matte canvas printed with UL- Greenguard Gold latex inks- Stretched on 1.25" radiata pine frame from FSC- renewable forests- Soft rubber dots on back to protect surfaces and keep canvas steady- Available in 60 sizes and in vertical, horizontal, or square orientations- Back hanging hardware included; size tolerance ±1/8" due to productionCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Painting Number 2 by Franz Kline Framed Abstract Canvas Art Print - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/painting-number-2-by-franz-kline-framed-abstract-canvas-art-print): Painting Number 2 by Franz Kline exemplifies the raw power of American Abstract Expressionism. Created during the height of Kline’s career in the early 1950s, the work reflects his radical shift from figurative studies to bold, black-and-white abstractions. Influenced by urban architecture, Japanese calligraphy, and his own enlarged sketches, Kline transformed spontaneous brushwork into monumental visual statements. Thick, gestural strokes surge across the canvas, conveying energy, tension, and immediacy. As a leading figure of the New York School, Kline redefined modern painting by embracing scale, movement, and emotional intensity, making Painting Number 2 a striking expression of post-war artistic freedom. This framed matte canvas brings calm and confident minimalism to a living space. Broad, gestural black strokes float across a warm off-white ground, creating a quiet rhythm that reads modern and meditative. The deep black frame gives the composition presence on the wall while the matte surface keeps reflections low and the texture subtle, so the painting-like marks feel tactile and intimate. It settles into a lounge, hallway, or workspace and holds the eye without shouting — a companion piece that anchors a neutral palette or balances a room of layered textures.Display it above a low credenza, beside a reading nook, or in an entry where guests first arrive. The restrained palette pairs easily with wood tones and soft leathers; the bold, abstract marks speak to those who appreciate contemporary art, architecture, and quiet design moments. Each print is produced with eco-minded materials and archival inks for lasting color and minimal glare, so the piece keeps its calm presence for years.Care: hang using the included sawtooth hardware; wipe dust with a soft, dry cloth.Product features- Greenguard Gold  latex inks for vivid, non-toxic color- Cotton–polyester composite canvas with proprietary matte coating- Sustainably sourced radial pine frame (FSC )- Multiple orientation and size options; sawtooth hanging hardware included- Matte finish and deep frame reduce glare and emphasize textureCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Painting Number 2 by Franz Kline Abstract Black and White Stretched Canvas Art – Matte Wall Decor - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/painting-number-2-by-franz-kline-abstract-black-and-white-stretched-canvas-art-matte-wall-decor): Franz Kline’s Painting Number 2 captures the uncompromising spirit of post-war American modernism. Emerging from the New York School, Kline became renowned for his dramatic black-and-white compositions that rejected traditional representation. This painting reflects his method of projecting small sketches onto large canvases, allowing instinctive gestures to dominate the surface. The bold contrasts and fractured forms evoke industrial landscapes and calligraphic rhythms without literal reference. Kline’s work embodied the energy of 1950s New York—a city rebuilding itself through art. Painting Number 2 stands as a powerful testament to Kline’s influence on Abstract Expressionism and his enduring legacy in contemporary art. A minimalist stretched canvas that brings quiet, abstract energy to modern interiors. Broad, gestural black strokes cross a soft ivory ground, creating a balanced tension that reads as both architectural and spontaneous. The 1.25" depth gives a substantial presence on the wall while the matte finish keeps glare low and the textures visible. It settles into living rooms, studios, and office walls where a restrained, contemplative statement is wanted—inviting viewers to pause and trace the layered marks. Hang it above a low console, beside a reading nook, or in a gallery wall grouping to add movement without overwhelming a calm palette.Product features- Matte, stretched canvas with 1.25" depth for a gallery-style presence- Printed with UL- Greenguard Gold latex inks for high image quality and low emissions- Stretched on radiata pine frame from FSC- renewable forests- Soft rubber dots and included back hanging hardware keep the canvas steady and easy to mount- Available in multiple orientations and sizes; slight size variance tolerance +/- 1/8" (3.2mm)Care instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Chief by Franz Kline Abstract Matte Canvas Framed Wall Art - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/chief-by-franz-kline-abstract-matte-canvas-framed-wall-art): Chief by Franz Kline is a commanding example of American Abstract Expressionism at its most forceful. Created during the 1950s, when Kline emerged as a leading figure of the New York School, the painting reflects his move toward monumental black-and-white compositions. Drawing inspiration from urban architecture, industrial forms, and Japanese calligraphy, Kline transformed spontaneous brushwork into structured yet dynamic imagery. The bold, assertive strokes of Chief convey authority, rhythm, and tension, echoing the post-war confidence of American art. This work exemplifies Kline’s mastery of scale and gesture, reinforcing his lasting influence on modern painting. Bold, tactile, and quietly dramatic — this matte framed canvas turns an arresting abstract into a contemplative focal point. Thick black brushstrokes move across a soft off-white ground, creating a rhythm that reads like a private sketch come to life. The deep matte surface soaks up light, giving the composition a graphite-like intimacy while the slim pine frame adds grounded structure and a modern edge. Hang it where conversations start: above a low sofa, at the end of a hallway, or leaning on a mantel to anchor a pared-back, art-forward room.Product features- Matte, multi-color printing on a cotton–polyester canvas composite- Printed with non-toxic latex inks and UL Greenguard Gold- inks- Stretched on FSC- radial pine frame with sawtooth hanging hardware- Available in multiple sizes and orientations (horizontal, vertical, square) with choice of frame colors- Sustainably produced materials and vivid, long-lasting color reproductionCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Chief by Franz Kline Abstract Matte Canvas Wall Art - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/chief-by-franz-kline-abstract-matte-canvas-wall-art): Franz Kline’s Chief embodies the raw energy that defined Abstract Expressionism in post-war New York. Known for projecting small sketches onto large canvases, Kline developed a distinctive visual language built on stark contrasts and powerful gestures. In Chief, thick black forms dominate the white surface, suggesting structure, movement, and dominance without direct representation. The painting reflects Kline’s interest in balance between spontaneity and control, a hallmark of his practice. As a key member of the New York School, Kline helped shift the centre of modern art to America, making Chief a bold statement of artistic authority. A bold, textured matte canvas that brings raw brushstrokes and deep contrasts to the wall. The stretched canvas sits on a slender 1.25" pine profile so the artwork floats with subtle depth. Heavy black gestures on a muted, warm ground create a contemplative, modern energy — the kind that anchors a living room corner, a quiet hallway, or a creative studio. Hang it slightly off-center above a console or let it converse with plants and ceramics on a gallery wall. The finish is velvety rather than glossy, keeping focus on the charcoal-like marks and the tactile surface of the paint. Each piece arrives ready to hang with soft rubber pads at the back to protect walls and a resilient inner pine frame for long-lasting tension and straight edges.Product features- Matte, velvety canvas printed with UL- Greenguard Gold inks for high image quality- Stretched on a 1.25" radiata pine frame sourced from FSC- renewable forests- Printed with non-toxic latex inks and finished with soft rubber pads to protect walls- Available in multiple orientations and sizes; back hanging hardware included- Slight size variation possible due to handmade stretching process (±1/8" tolerance)Care instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [New York 7th AV attributed to Voke Framed Matte Canvas – Art Print - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/new-york-7th-av-attributed-to-voke-framed-matte-canvas-art-print): New York 7th Ave, attributed to Voke, captures the dynamic energy of mid-20th-century urban life. Little is firmly documented about the artist, a fact that lends the work an air of intrigue and discovery. The painting reflects a modernist sensibility, portraying the rhythm of New York City through bold composition and atmospheric depth. Seventh Avenue, long associated with commerce, fashion, and constant movement, becomes a symbol of metropolitan vitality. Executed with confident brushwork and an eye for structure, the work evokes a city in perpetual motion, situating the painting within a broader tradition of artists responding to New York’s architectural scale and modern pace. A vivid matte canvas print framed in a sleek black pine frame that brings a bustling city street to life. The textured canvas coating and latex inks produce rich, saturated hues — fiery oranges, deep reds, and cool urban blues — that keep every brushstroke visible and dynamic. Hung in a living room, studio, or office, this piece sparks conversation and draws viewers into a moment of city motion: taxis, pedestrians, and neon hints captured in an impressionistic rush. Sustainably made materials and sawtooth hardware make it easy to display responsibly and securely.Product features- Greenguard Gold  latex inks for safe, high-fidelity color- Cotton–polyester composite canvas with proprietary coating for sharp detail- Sustainably sourced radial pine frame with multiple size and color options- Vibrant, long-lasting color reproduction using latest printing techniques- Sawtooth hanging hardware included for easy wall installationCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [New York 7th AV attributed to Voke Canvas Wall Art — Matte Stretched Canvas - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/new-york-7th-av-attributed-to-voke-canvas-wall-art-matte-stretched-canvas): Attributed to the elusive artist Voke, New York 7th Ave presents a compelling vision of one of Manhattan’s most iconic thoroughfares. The scarcity of biographical information surrounding Voke heightens the painting’s appeal, allowing the work itself to take centre stage. Influenced by urban modernism, the composition emphasises perspective, movement, and the density of city life. Seventh Avenue is rendered as both a physical location and a metaphor for ambition and progress. With its expressive handling and metropolitan subject matter, the painting aligns with mid-century interpretations of New York as the beating heart of modern culture. A vibrant stretched canvas that brings city energy into any room. Bold strokes of crimson, gold, and teal capture a sunlit intersection—taxis, traffic lights, and people in motion—rendered with painterly texture and cinematic contrast. The matte finish softens glare while preserving color depth, and the 1.25" pine profile gives a clean, gallery-style edge that sits neatly on the wall. Hang it above a sofa, in an entryway, or in a compact apartment to anchor the space with movement and warmth. The image feels like a snapshot of rush-hour hustle slowed down into a luminous, contemplative moment.Product features- Matte, non-reflective finish for true-to-art color- Stretched on 1.25" radiata pine frame with clean gallery edge- Printed with UL- Greenguard Gold latex inks (non-toxic)- Soft rubber dots on back to keep canvas steady when hung- Available in multiple orientations and sizes; back-hanging hardware includedCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Vleken by an unknown artist Framed Canvas Art - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/vleken-by-an-unknown-artist-framed-canvas-art): Vleken by an unknown artist is an evocative work whose title suggests marks, stains, or impressions, possibly derived from the Dutch word vlek, meaning “spot” or “mark.” The absence of an identified artist invites close attention to the painting itself, allowing form, texture, and mood to take precedence. The composition appears to explore abstraction through layered surfaces and expressive gestures, encouraging open interpretation. Such works reflect a broader modern tradition in which meaning is conveyed through visual impact rather than narrative. Vleken stands as a compelling reminder that artistic power often transcends authorship, relying instead on emotion and material presence. A framed matte canvas that brings calm energy to any room. Soft layered brushstrokes in muted blues, warm browns, and charcoal form an abstract woodland rhythm — like a quiet morning walk captured in paint. The slim pine frame and subtle matte finish keep the focus on texture and color, while the archival coating and vibrant latex inks hold the mood true over time. Hang it above a mantel, beside a reading nook, or in a minimalist hallway to deepen a space with gentle, nature-inspired color and quiet motion.Product features- Greenguard Gold  inks for high-quality, low-emission prints- Cotton-polyester canvas with proprietary matte coating for rich detail- Sustainably sourced radial pine frame in multiple color options- Durable, non-toxic latex inks for vivid, lasting color- Includes sawtooth hanging hardware; available in multiple orientations and sizesCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Vleken by an unknown artist Abstract Matte Canvas Wall Art - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/vleken-by-an-unknown-artist-abstract-matte-canvas-wall-art): Attributed to an unknown artist, Vleken possesses a quiet intrigue rooted in both its title and anonymity. The word suggests traces or marks, aligning with the painting’s emphasis on surface, movement, and visual rhythm. Without a documented creator, the work invites the viewer to engage directly with its abstract qualities, free from biographical framing. The painting resonates with mid-20th-century modernist sensibilities, where experimentation and material expression took precedence over representation. Vleken appeals to collectors drawn to mystery and form, offering a visually engaging piece that allows personal interpretation to shape its meaning.A matte stretched canvas with muted, painterly marks that brings quiet movement to a room. The soft palette of slate blue, warm taupe and charcoal creates an organic, layered composition — like walking through a foggy grove after rain. Hung above a sofa, in a hallway, or on a bedroom wall, this canvas adds calm texture without shouting for attention. The narrow 1.25" profile keeps the focus on the art while the coated surface preserves rich, detailed ink work. It’s an understated statement that complements neutral, Scandinavian, and modern rustic interiors, and settles easily into seasonal refreshes or room-by-room updates.Product features- High-quality matte coating with UL- Greenguard Gold latex inks- Stretched on renewable radiata pine frame; available in vertical, horizontal, and square sizes- Durable cotton-poly canvas composite with soft rubber dots to protect surfaces- Back hanging hardware included; inner frame profile 1.25" thick- Available in 60 size options; slight size tolerance +/- 1/8" due to productionCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Mediterranean Street Scene Matte Canvas Framed Wall Art - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/mediterranean-street-scene-matte-canvas-framed-wall-art): Painted in 1888 during Vincent van Gogh’s transformative stay in Arles, The Yellow House captures the artist’s dream of creating a vibrant community of painters in the south of France. The building depicted was Van Gogh’s own rented home, intended as a shared studio for artists such as Paul Gauguin. Using bold outlines and radiant yellows, Van Gogh infused the scene with emotional intensity and symbolic warmth. The work reflects his fascination with light, colour harmony, and Japanese prints, while also revealing his longing for stability and artistic companionship during a pivotal moment in his career. Sunlit alleys and warm plaster walls come alive on matte canvas. This framed canvas print captures a quiet European corner—golden facades, a red-tiled roof, and a cobalt sky—rendered with painterly texture and vivid, archival color. The slender pine frame and matte finish give the piece a refined, gallery-ready presence while keeping the surface soft and glare-free. It slips naturally into morning rooms, reading nooks, and cozy cafés, bringing a calm, lived-in warmth to the wall.Place it above a console, beside a bookshelf, or over a small bistro table to extend that late-afternoon light into your home. The sustainable pine frame and durable, low-VOC inks mean the print looks fresh for years without fuss. Available in multiple orientations and sizes, this framed matte canvas adapts to tight spots or larger arrangements in layered gallery walls.Product features- Greenguard Gold latex inks for vivid, non-toxic color- Proprietary cotton/polyester canvas with special matte coating- Sustainably sourced radial pine frame from FSC- wood- Multiple sizes and horizontal/vertical/square orientations available- Sawtooth hanging hardware included for easy displayCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [The Yellow House by Vincent van Gogh Matte Canvas Art – Stretched 1.25" - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/the-yellow-house-by-vincent-van-gogh-matte-canvas-art-stretched-1-25): The Yellow House is a defining work from Vincent van Gogh’s Arles period, when his use of colour became increasingly expressive and symbolic. Painted in 1888, the scene depicts the modest house that embodied Van Gogh’s ambition to establish an artists’ collective in Provence. The intense yellows and contrasting blues convey optimism and creative energy, while the simplified forms echo his admiration for Japanese aesthetics. More than a cityscape, the painting represents Van Gogh’s personal vision of belonging and artistic renewal, created shortly before the emotional turmoil that would shape the final years of his life. Warm sunlight spills across sun-washed facades in this stretched matte canvas print, bringing a quiet European street scene into your room. Brushstrokes of ochre, mint, and terracotta create a lived-in, painterly warmth that feels like a slow afternoon at a sidewalk café. The 1.25" depth gives the piece gentle presence on the wall without overwhelming a cozy corner. Hang it above a reading nook, lean it on a gallery ledge, or place it where soft natural light can accentuate the textured matte surface—this canvas settles into spaces that value calm, color, and the comfortable rhythm of everyday life.Product features- High-quality matte canvas printed with UL- Greenguard Gold inks for vivid, safe color- Stretched on renewable radiata pine frame with a 1.25" profile for a clean, substantial look- Durable cotton/poly canvas printed with non-toxic latex inks- Soft rubber dots and included back hanging hardware for secure, level display- Available in multiple orientations and many sizes; slight size tolerance ±1/8" due to productionCare instructions- If the canvas does gather any dust, you may wipe it off gently with a clean, damp cloth. - Maison Abstract

- [Abstract Earth-Tone Pattern Mug — Neutral Watercolor Dots & Strokes 11oz - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/abstract-earth-tone-pattern-mug-neutral-watercolor-dots-strokes-11oz): A handcrafted-feel ceramic mug wrapped in an abstract watercolor of muted grays, warm browns, and smoky blues. Hold it by the comfortable C-handle as the glaze catches the light and small, painterly details reveal themselves with each sip. Attributed to an unknown artist, Vleken possesses a quiet intrigue rooted in both its title and anonymity. The word suggests traces or marks, aligning with the painting’s emphasis on surface, movement, and visual rhythm. Without a documented creator, the work invites the viewer to engage directly with its abstract qualities, free from biographical framing. The painting resonates with mid-20th-century modernist sensibilities, where experimentation and material expression took precedence over representation. Vleken appeals to collectors drawn to mystery and form, offering a visually engaging piece that allows personal interpretation to shape its meaning. A leading figure in Swiss modernism, Hodler sought universal truths through rhythm, symmetry, and repetition—principles he called “parallelism.” In this work, the unified figures embody collective harmony and shared purpose, transcending individual identity. Painted during a period when Hodler was increasingly recognised across Europe, Einmütigkeit expresses his belief in spiritual order and human solidarity. The restrained palette and monumental forms reinforce the painting’s meditative power, making it a defining example of Hodler’s symbolic language. - [1966 World Cup Final Football Mug — Retro Soccer Match Scene 11oz - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/1966-world-cup-final-vintage-football-mug-retro-soccer-match-scene-11oz): The 1966 FIFA World Cup stands as one of the most iconic moments in football history. Hosted by England, the tournament reached its climax at Wembley Stadium on 30 July 1966, where England faced West Germany in the final. Before a sea of passionate supporters, the teams entered the pitch amid an atmosphere of ceremony and expectation. England, wearing their classic red shirts, went on to secure a dramatic 4–2 victory after extra time, claiming their first and only World Cup title. Remembered for its intensity, controversy, and national pride, the 1966 World Cup remains a defining chapter in English football heritage. Steam rises from a freshly brewed cup as the team files onto the pitch. This 11oz white ceramic mug wraps a vintage match-day moment around your morning ritual — the roar of the crowd, the worn red kit, the quiet focus before kickoff. It slips easily into a commuter’s hand, a collector’s shelf, or a weekend routine where memories of old matches are retold. Each sip becomes a small trip back to terraces and halftime talks — familiar, warm, and lived-in. For fans who live for retro football culture, this mug speaks the language of matchday nostalgia. The slightly grainy, period-accurate photo keeps the era honest: scarves, packed stands, and boots that knew mud. Use it during game-watching gatherings, vintage-themed nights, or quiet mornings when you’re sketching lineups or replaying classic goals in your head. The image feels like a snapshot pulled from an album — worn around the edges but full of life. This piece works well alongside team scarves, old pennants, and a stack of match programs. It’s for anyone who prefers the textures of history to modern gloss — those who savor the stories behind each crest and replay. Hold it, drink from it, and let one of football’s slower, dustier afternoons set the tone for your day. Product features - 11 oz white ceramic with comfortable C-handle - Dishwasher-safe for easy cleaning - Microwave-safe for reheating drinks - Vibrant, long-lasting print with AAA+ ORCA coating - Durable finish that preserves color and luster over time Care instructions - Clean in dishwasher or wash by hand with warm water and dish soap - Maison Abstract

- [Pop Art Woman Graphic T‑Shirt — Retro Chic Green Glasses Design - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/pop-art-woman-graphic-t-shirt-retro-chic-green-glasses-design): Created by Orit Fuchs, a contemporary abstract artist known for her expressive use of colour, Vivid 2 exemplifies an intuitive and emotionally driven approach to painting. Fuchs draws on influences from modern abstraction, allowing colour relationships and painterly gestures to guide the composition. The work’s layered textures and saturated hues generate depth and movement, encouraging personal interpretation. Rather than fixed narrative, Vivid 2 offers an open visual dialogue, reflecting the artist’s belief in abstraction as a direct emotional experience. The painting stands as a celebration of spontaneity, energy, and expressive freedom. This heavyweight, garment-dyed tee brings bold pop-art energy to everyday wear. The relaxed fit and soft, washed-in color come together with a striking portrait print — oversized green frames, glossy lips, and a confident, playful expression — so the shirt reads like wearable attitude. It feels lived-in from the first wear: softened cotton, robust stitching, and a roomy cut that layers easily under jackets or stands alone as the outfit statement. Slip it on for gallery nights, coffee runs, or weekends of people-watching; the design sparks conversation while the durable construction keeps it in rotation season after season.Product features- 100% ring-spun US cotton for lasting comfort- Garment-dyed finish for a soft, vintage-wash look- Heavyweight fabric (6.1 oz/yd²) with relaxed fit- Double-needle stitched seams and tubular knit (no side seams) for durability- Sizes S–4XL with pre-shrunk fabric and sewn-in labelCare instructions- Machine wash: cold (max 30C or 90F)- Do not bleach- Tumble dry: low heat- Iron, steam or dry: low heat- Do not dryclean - Maison Abstract

- [Artistic Pop Portrait T-Shirt — Colorful Marilyn-Inspired Graphic Tee - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/artistic-pop-portrait-t-shirt-colorful-marilyn-inspired-graphic-tee): This heavyweight garment-dyed tee brings gallery energy to everyday wear. The bold, painterly portrait print reads like a modern pop-art masterpiece — vivid blues, fiery reds, and textured brushstrokes that catch the eye without shouting. The relaxed, slightly boxy fit and soft, garment-dyed finish give it a lived-in feel from day one. Wear it layered under a denim jacket for city outings, paired with relaxed jeans for weekend wandering, or solo when you want your outfit to do the talking. It’s for people who collect visual moments and like their clothes to feel worn-in, comfortable, and unmistakably expressive.magnetic charm of Marilyn Monroe, reimagined through bold colour and expressive brushwork. Bursting with vibrant reds, electric blues and warm golden tones, the artwork transforms the legendary Hollywood icon into a modern pop-art masterpiece. Voke’s dynamic layering and confident strokes create a sense of movement and emotional intensity, giving the classic muse a fresh, energetic presence. Perfect for modern interiors, studios and statement walls, this piece captures the allure, strength and cinematic legacy of Marilyn Monroe while adding a dramatic, eye-catching focal point to any space. Product features- 100% ring-spun US cotton for lasting comfort- Heavyweight fabric (6.1 oz/yd²) with a relaxed fit- Garment-dyed for soft color and lived-in texture- Double-needle stitched seams and tubular (no side seams) construction- Sizes S–4XL available; pre-shrunk with sewn-in labelCare instructions- Machine wash: cold (max 30C or 90F)- Do not bleach- Tumble dry: low heat- Iron, steam or dry: low heat- Do not dryclean - Maison Abstract

- [Art Portrait Tote Bag — Serene Red-Hooded Figure Canvas Cotton Tote - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/products/art-portrait-tote-bag-serene-red-hooded-figure-canvas-cotton-tote): A quiet, contemplative cotton tote that carries more than just your essentials. The printed portrait—softly painted, timeless and a touch mysterious—sits against deep teal and warm red tones that catch the eye without shouting. Lightweight and smooth to the touch, this tote folds easily into a bag for market runs, a book, or a sketchpad. The reinforced handles and neat flat corners keep it feeling reliable, while double-sided printing lets the image speak from every angle. It sits naturally in moments of slow travel, gallery visits, or when you want a small, personal piece of art with you on errands or quiet afternoons.Odilon Redon’s Le Silence showcases the Symbolist painter’s mastery of mood and atmosphere. A central figure in late 19th-century French art, Redon sought to depict the emotional and spiritual realm rather than the visible world. Created during a period when his palette shifted toward soft, radiant colours, this piece embodies the tranquillity and introspection that define his mature style. The gentle hues and delicate shading evoke a meditative stillness that invites viewers inward. This high-quality reproduction introduces a refined, contemplative presence to any space, perfect for admirers of Symbolism, modernist colour work, and expressive, dreamlike imagery.Product features- 100% cotton — smooth, strong fabric suited for vivid printing- Reinforced-stitched handles for extra durability- Design printed on both sides for full visual impact- Extra-lightweight fabric (140 g/m²) and one-size for easy folding and carrying- Flat-corner construction for a slim, artfully finished silhouetteCare instructions- Hand wash only. Let the bag air dry - Maison Abstract

- [František Kupka - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/frantisek-kupka): František Kupka – Pioneer of Abstract ArtFrantišek Kupka (1871–1957) was a visionary Czech painter and one of the earliest founders of abstract art. Trained in Prague and Paris, Kupka moved beyond traditional realism to explore color, rhythm, and movement as pure expression. His works, including The Yellow Scale and Amorpha: Fugue in Two Colors, reveal a deep fascination with music, science, and spiritual energy. A leading figure of the Parisian avant-garde, Kupka’s bold experimentation reshaped twentieth-century modernism. His art embodies freedom, emotion, and innovation — a perfect choice for collectors and design lovers seeking meaningful, timeless wall art rooted in history and creativity. - Maison Abstract

- [Edvard Munch - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/edvard-munch): Edvard Munch (1863–1944) was a pioneering Norwegian painter whose emotionally charged style helped shape modern art. Best known for The Scream, Munch explored universal themes of love, anxiety, and mortality through bold color, expressive brushwork, and haunting symbolism. His work bridged Impressionism and Expressionism, influencing generations of artists with its raw psychological depth. Deeply personal and introspective, Munch’s art reflects his lifelong struggle with emotion and identity. From serene landscapes to powerful portraits, his paintings capture the beauty and tension of the human condition—making his work timeless and deeply moving for any art lover or collector. - Maison Abstract

- [Edgar Degas - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/edgar-degas): Edgar Degas (1834–1917) was a French Impressionist master celebrated for his elegant depictions of ballet dancers, daily life, and movement. Though often associated with Impressionism, Degas preferred to call himself a realist, focusing on form, composition, and the subtleties of human gesture. His innovative use of pastels and dynamic viewpoints brought energy and intimacy to his work. Fascinated by motion, he frequently portrayed dancers, bathers, and horse riders in candid, unposed moments. Degas’s artistry blends precision with emotion, transforming ordinary scenes into timeless studies of grace and rhythm. His legacy continues to inspire generations of modern and contemporary artists. - Maison Abstract

Krøyer (1851–1909) was one of Denmark’s most celebrated painters and a leading member of the Skagen artists’ colony. Renowned for his luminous depictions of seaside life, Krøyer mastered the delicate “blue hour” light that became a hallmark of his work. His paintings capture friendship, community, and the calm beauty of Nordic summers with remarkable realism and atmosphere. Influenced by French Impressionism, Krøyer blended technical skill with a uniquely Scandinavian mood. Today, his art remains internationally admired for its serenity and elegance, making Krøyer’s work a timeless choice for collectors and interior spaces. - Maison Abstract

- [William Adolphe Bouguereau - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/william-adolphe-bouguereau): William-Adolphe Bouguereau was a leading 19th-century French academic painter, renowned for his exceptional realism, classical technique, and idealised figurative works. Trained at the École des Beaux-Arts in Paris, Bouguereau achieved international acclaim for his religious, mythological, and genre paintings, distinguished by flawless anatomy, luminous skin tones, and refined composition. A master of academic classicism, he remained committed to traditional art during the rise of Impressionism. Today, Bouguereau’s paintings are celebrated for their technical brilliance, emotional depth, and timeless beauty, making him one of the most influential and collectible artists of the academic art movement. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:53Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Kitano Tsunetomi - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/kitano-tsunetomi): Kitano Tsunetomi (1878–1947) was a leading Japanese Nihonga painter renowned for his refined bijin-ga (portraits of beautiful women). Active during Japan’s transition from the Meiji to early Shōwa periods, Tsunetomi blended classical Japanese aesthetics with subtle modern realism. Based in Osaka, he became a central figure in Kansai art circles, elevating Nihonga through elegant line work, delicate colour harmonies, and psychological depth. His paintings often depict women in quiet, intimate moments, reflecting both traditional culture and contemporary life. Today, Kitano Tsunetomi is celebrated for preserving Japanese artistic heritage while introducing a sophisticated, modern sensibility. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:21Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Max Klinger - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/max-klinger): Max Klinger (1857–1920) was a pioneering German Symbolist artist renowned for his expressive prints, paintings, and sculptures. Best known for his ambitious graphic cycles such as Paraphrase on the Finding of a Glove, Klinger explored themes of dreams, desire, mythology, and the subconscious. Working at the crossroads of realism and symbolism, he influenced major figures including Edvard Munch, Giorgio de Chirico, and Käthe Kollwitz. Klinger's innovative use of etching and printmaking expanded the expressive potential of graphic art, securing his legacy as a central figure in late nineteenth-century European modernism. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:22Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Winold Reiss - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/winold-reiss): Winold Reiss was a German-born American artist best known for his expressive portraiture and influential role in early 20th-century modern art. Emigrating to the United States in 1913, Reiss became renowned for capturing cultural identity, dignity, and individuality across diverse communities, including Native American, African American, and immigrant subjects. His distinctive style blends European modernism with realism, emphasising strong line, subtle colour, and psychological depth. Beyond portrait painting, Reiss contributed to mural design, illustration, and art education, shaping American visual culture. Today, Winold Reiss is celebrated for his humanistic vision and enduring impact on modern portrait art. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:22Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Coffee Mugs - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/coffee-mugs): Discover the Artistic Coffee Mug Collection by Maison Abstract, where everyday rituals meet contemporary art. Each mug is designed as a miniature canvas, blending bold abstraction, refined colour palettes, and modern aesthetics. Crafted for both beauty and durability, these mugs elevate your coffee or tea experience while making a statement on any table or workspace. Inspired by gallery-worthy artworks and creative expression, the collection celebrates individuality, design, and mindful living. Whether styled in your kitchen or gifted to a design lover, Maison Abstract’s artistic mugs transform simple moments into curated experiences—where art is not only admired, but used every day. - Maison Abstract

- [Ferdinand Hodler - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/ferdinand-hodler): Ferdinand Hodler was a pioneering Swiss painter and one of the most influential figures of European Symbolism and early modern art. Known for his powerful use of symmetry, rhythm, and repetition, Hodler developed a unique artistic philosophy he called “parallelism,” expressing universal themes of life, death, unity, and spirituality. Rising from a childhood marked by hardship, Hodler achieved international recognition in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. His monumental figurative works, landscapes, and allegorical paintings remain highly sought after by collectors and museums, securing his lasting legacy in the history of modern art. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:53Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Tote Bags - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/tote-bags): Discover the Artistic Tote Bag Collection by Maison Abstract, where functional design meets contemporary art. Each tote is conceived as a wearable canvas, featuring bold abstract compositions, refined colour harmonies, and modern visual language. Crafted for everyday use with durable materials and thoughtful proportions, these bags balance practicality with artistic expression. Inspired by gallery culture and creative individuality, the collection transforms an essential accessory into a statement piece. Perfect for daily errands, travel, or gifting, Maison Abstract tote bags celebrate art in motion—designed for those who carry creativity with them wherever they go. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-04-20T16:25:29Z
  Total Products: 15
- [Cyprien Eugène Boule - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/cyprien-eugene-boule): Cyprien Eugène Boulet was a 19th-century French painter known for his refined portraits and elegant figure studies rooted in academic tradition. His work reflects a deep sensitivity to mood, texture, and human expression, often highlighting luxurious fabrics and calm, contemplative poses. Boulet’s paintings embody the artistic values of late-19th-century France, balancing realism with subtle romanticism. Today, his art is appreciated for its timeless beauty, technical precision, and intimate atmosphere. Collectors and art enthusiasts value Cyprien Eugène Boulet for his graceful compositions, soft color palettes, and his ability to transform simple portrait subjects into enduring works of classical art history. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:54Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Katsushika Hokusai - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/katsushika-hokusai): Katsushika Hokusai was a pioneering Japanese ukiyo-e artist best known for The Great Wave off Kanagawa, one of the most recognisable artworks in history. Born in Edo (Tokyo) in 1760, Hokusai produced thousands of woodblock prints, drawings, and paintings over a prolific career spanning more than 70 years. His innovative compositions, dynamic line work, and bold use of colour—particularly Prussian blue—redefined Japanese landscape art. Hokusai’s Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji series secured his international reputation and strongly influenced Western artists such as Van Gogh and Monet. Today, Hokusai remains a central figure in global art history and Japanese culture. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:54Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Johannes Handschin - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/johannes-handschin): Johannes Handschin was a Swiss Symbolist painter and illustrator known for his refined Art Nouveau style and poetic visual language. Active in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, Handschin created dreamlike compositions rich in allegory, mythology, and decorative detail. His work reflects the Symbolist movement’s fascination with emotion, spirituality, and the inner world, often featuring elegant figures, floral motifs, and harmonious color palettes. Influenced by European Jugendstil and fin-de-siècle aesthetics, Johannes Handschin’s art remains admired for its lyrical beauty and craftsmanship. Today, his artworks are sought after by collectors of Symbolist and Art Nouveau prints. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-02-15T01:47:54Z
  Total Products: 0
- [Dorothy Charleton Smyth - Maison Abstract](https://maison-abstract.com/collections/dorothy-charleton-smyth): Dorothy Charleton Smyth was a pioneering British artist and educator known for her refined portraits, still lifes, and commitment to advancing women in the arts. Active in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, Smyth studied in Paris and exhibited widely at the Royal Academy and international salons. Her work blends academic precision with subtle modern sensibility, capturing character, mood, and quiet elegance. As the first woman to serve as Director of the Glasgow School of Art, she played a vital role in shaping British art education. Today, Dorothy Charleton Smyth is celebrated for her artistic legacy and influence. - Maison Abstract
  Updated: 2026-04-20T16:25:29Z
  Total Products: 0

  - [Minimalist Art Prints for a Calm Home](https://maison-abstract.com/blogs/abstract-view-on-life/blog-minimalist-art-prints): <p>In a world that feels increasingly loud, fast, and cluttered, many people are seeking refuge in calmer, more intentional living spaces. Our homes are no longer just places to sleep; they are sanctuaries where we recharge, think, and reconnect with ourselves. One of the most powerful yet understated ways to shape a peaceful interior is through art. In particular, <strong data-start="410" data-end="435">minimalist art prints</strong> have become a defining feature of calm, modern homes, offering visual breathing room without sacrificing personality or depth.</p>
    Updated: 2026-02-12T11:54:39Z
